// Status: Security Layer
// Purpose: Hybrid Post-Quantum Cryptography (Kyber768 + X25519).
// Dependencies: x25519-dalek, pqcrypto-kyber, pqcrypto-traits, sha2, hkdf, rand

use crate::error::{FusionError, Result};
use rand::rngs::OsRng;
use x25519_dalek::{StaticSecret, PublicKey};
use pqcrypto_kyber::kyber768;
use pqcrypto_traits::kem::{Ciphertext, SecretKey as KyberSecret, PublicKey as KyberPublic};
use pqcrypto_traits::kem::SharedSecret;
use sha2::Sha256;
use hkdf::Hkdf;

/// A Hybrid Public Key containing both ECC and Lattice components.
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct HybridPublicKey {
    pub ecc_key: PublicKey,
    pub lattice_key: KyberPublic,
}

impl HybridPublicKey {
    pub fn to_bytes(&self) -> Vec<u8> {
        let mut bytes = Vec::new();
        bytes.extend_from_slice(self.ecc_key.as_bytes());
        bytes.extend_from_slice(self.lattice_key.as_bytes());
        bytes
    }
}

/// A Hybrid Secret Key.
/// Use `drop` (or zeroize trait) to clear memory in production.
pub struct HybridSecretKey {
    pub ecc_secret: StaticSecret,
    pub lattice_secret: KyberSecret,
}

impl HybridSecretKey {
    /// Generates a new ephemeral keypair using the OS CSPRNG.
    pub fn generate() -> (Self, HybridPublicKey) {
        // 1. Generate Classical ECC Keypair
        let ecc_secret = StaticSecret::random_from_rng(OsRng);
        let ecc_public = PublicKey::from(&ecc_secret);

        // 2. Generate Post-Quantum Kyber Keypair
        let (lat_pub, lat_sec) = kyber768::keypair();

        (
            Self {
                ecc_secret,
                lattice_secret: lat_sec,
            },
            HybridPublicKey {
                ecc_key: ecc_public,
                lattice_key: lat_pub,
            }
        )
    }

    /// Decapsulates a shared secret sent by a peer.
    ///
    /// This combines the ECDH shared secret and the Kyber shared secret
    /// using a KDF to ensure that if *either* algorithm is broken,
    /// the session remains secure (as long as the other holds).
    ///
    /// # Arguments
    /// * `ciphertext`: The Kyber ciphertext (1088 bytes for Kyber768).
    /// * `peer_ecc`: The peer's X25519 public key.
    pub fn decapsulate(&self, ciphertext_bytes: &[u8], peer_ecc: &PublicKey) -> Result<[u8; 32]> {
        // 1. Reconstruct Ciphertext object (validation)
        let ciphertext = Ciphertext::from_bytes(ciphertext_bytes)
            .map_err(|_| FusionError::CryptoFailure { 
                reason: "Invalid Kyber ciphertext format".into(), 
                fatal: true 
            })?;

        // 2. Perform X25519 Diffie-Hellman
        let ecc_shared_secret = self.ecc_secret.diffie_hellman(peer_ecc);

        // 3. Perform Kyber Decapsulation
        let kyber_shared_secret = kyber768::decapsulate(&ciphertext, &self.lattice_secret);

        // 4. Hybrid KDF (HKDF-SHA256)
        // Design Rationale: We concatenate the two secrets.
        // Input Key Material (IKM) = ECDH_Secret || Kyber_Secret
        let mut ikm = Vec::new();
        ikm.extend_from_slice(ecc_shared_secret.as_bytes());
        ikm.extend_from_slice(kyber_shared_secret.as_bytes());

        let hkdf = Hkdf::<Sha256>::new(None, &ikm);
        
        let mut final_session_key = [0u8; 32];
        hkdf.expand(b"Fusion-v3-Hybrid-Session", &mut final_session_key)
            .map_err(|_| FusionError::CryptoFailure { 
                reason: "KDF Expansion failed".into(), 
                fatal: true 
            })?;

        Ok(final_session_key)
    }
}
